days.label: Upper
days.title: Upper Body — Strength Base
days.subtitle: Heavy compounds first. Balanced push/pull. Arms and abs.

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.tip: Pause half-second at chest to eliminate momentum. Eyes under bar, slight arch, forearms vertical at bottom. Elbows 45-75°.

alternativeTips.DB Bench Press: Greater ROM and stretch at the bottom. Rotate palms slightly inward. Lower to chest level — the deeper stretch drives more growth.
alternativeTips.Machine Chest Press: Focus on squeezing at full contraction. Use controlled negatives to compensate for the fixed path.

formTips: Retract and depress shoulder blades before unracking
Wrists straight — bar sits on heel of palm, not bent back
Drive feet into floor for leg drive
Bar path: slight diagonal from chest to over shoulders
commonMistakes: Bouncing bar off chest
Flaring elbows to 90 degrees
Lifting hips off bench

exercises.name: Cable Row (Close Grip)
exercises.tip: Pull with elbows, not hands — imagine hands are hooks. Let weight stretch you forward at bottom. Squeeze shoulder blades for a full second at peak.
formTips: Pull elbows straight back, not outward
Let shoulders protract fully at the stretch
Squeeze shoulder blades together for 1 second at peak
commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight
Shrugging shoulders up instead of pulling back
Cutting range of motion short at the stretch

exercises.name: Seated DB Shoulder Press
exercises.tip: Bench at 80-85°. Lower to ear level minimum every rep — the stretched position at the bottom is where growth stimulus is highest.
formTips: Set bench to 80-85°, not fully vertical
Lower dumbbells to at least ear level every rep
Press up and slightly inward
commonMistakes: Bench set too upright (turns into a trap exercise)
Not lowering deep enough — cutting ROM
Arching lower back excessively

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)
exercises.tip: Lean back 15-20°. Let shoulders fully elevate at top — the stretch is the most important part for lat growth.
formTips: Lean back 15-20° and hold that angle
Let shoulders fully elevate at the top
Pull bar to upper chest, not behind neck
commonMistakes: Pulling behind the neck (shoulder injury risk)
Not letting arms fully extend at top
Using body momentum to swing the weight down

exercises.name: Mid Chest Cable Fly
exercises.tip: Lock elbow angle throughout. Try crossing hands slightly at peak for extra inner chest squeeze.
formTips: Lock slight bend in elbows throughout
Pulleys at chest height
Squeeze and hold at peak 1 second
commonMistakes: Bending elbows too much (turns it into a press)
Using too much weight and losing control

exercises.name: DB Curl
exercises.tip: Pin elbows to sides. The 3-second lowering drives growth more than adding 10 lbs with sloppy form.
formTips: Pin elbows to your sides throughout
Lower over 3 full seconds every rep
Supinate (rotate pinky up) at the top for peak contraction
commonMistakes: Swinging the weight up with momentum
Letting elbows drift forward
Rushing the eccentric (lowering) phase

exercises.name: Tricep Rope Extension
exercises.tip: Split rope apart at bottom and hold — targets lateral head for arm width. Lock elbows at sides throughout.
formTips: Lock elbows at your sides — they should not move
Split the rope apart at the bottom and squeeze
Controlled return, don't let the stack slam
commonMistakes: Elbows drifting forward (turns into a press)
Not splitting the rope at full extension
Using too much weight and losing elbow position

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.tip: Curl pelvis up (belt buckle to chin). Without the pelvic tilt, hip flexors do 80% of the work.
formTips: Curl pelvis up — belt buckle toward chin
Control the descent, no swinging
Keep legs together throughout
commonMistakes: Just lifting legs without pelvic tilt (hip flexors dominate)
Using momentum and swinging
Letting legs drop uncontrolled

days.label: Lower
days.title: Lower Body — Strength Base
days.subtitle: Squat and deadlift pattern. Quad/ham balance. Calves and abs.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.tip: Hip crease below knee. Big belly breath, brace core. Knees track over toes. Film from the side to verify depth.

alternativeTips.Hack Squat: More quad-dominant due to fixed back angle. Go as deep as possible. Feet lower on platform = more quad.
alternativeTips.Leg Press: 90° knee bend minimum. Lower foot placement = more quad, higher = more glute. Never lock knees at the top.
alternativeTips.Goblet Squat: Hold dumbbell at chest. Great for learning depth. Limited by upper body fatigue — use for technique days.

formTips: Brace core before descent — belly breath into belt
Feet shoulder-width, toes out 15-30° — find your natural angle
Knees track over toes, push out slightly
Control descent, explode up
commonMistakes: Knees caving inward
Rising hips first (good morning squat)
Not hitting proper depth

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.tip: Hips higher than knees (not a squat). Use high handles now, progress to low handles for more ROM over time.
formTips: Hips higher than knees — it's a hinge, not a squat
Drive through the floor, keep chest up
Lockout by squeezing glutes, not hyperextending back
commonMistakes: Squatting the weight up instead of hinging
Rounding lower back under load
Jerking the bar off the floor instead of smooth pull

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.tip: Front shin vertical at bottom. Rear foot for balance only. Upright torso = more quad. Slight lean = more glute.
formTips: Front shin vertical at the bottom position
Rear foot on bench for balance only — don't push off it
Control the descent, pause briefly at the bottom
commonMistakes: Stepping too close to the bench (knee shoots forward)
Pushing off the rear foot
Leaning too far forward with heavy load

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.tip: Full lockout, squeeze quads 1 second every rep. Knees aligned with machine pivot.
formTips: Full lockout at the top, squeeze quads hard
Align knees with the machine pivot point
Controlled lowering — don't let the stack slam
commonMistakes: Not fully extending the legs at the top
Using momentum to swing the weight
Knees misaligned with machine pivot

exercises.name: Leg Curl
exercises.tip: Point toes away (plantarflexion) to reduce calf involvement. Hamstrings respond exceptionally to slow 3-second negatives.
formTips: Point toes away from you to isolate hamstrings
Lower over 3 full seconds every rep
Curl as close to glutes as possible
commonMistakes: Rushing the eccentric phase
Lifting hips off the pad
Not curling through full range of motion

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.tip: Drop heels as low as possible, rise as high as possible. Pause both ends. Targets soleus (60% of calf size).
formTips: Drop heels as low as possible for full stretch
Rise as high as possible and pause at the top
Pause at the bottom stretch for 1-2 seconds
commonMistakes: Bouncing through reps without pausing
Cutting range of motion short
Going too heavy and losing control

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.tip: Hips locked — don't sit back. Forceful exhale at bottom increases contraction 20-30%.
formTips: Keep hips locked in place throughout
Forceful exhale at the bottom of each rep
Think ribcage moving toward pelvis
commonMistakes: Sitting back onto heels (hip flexion, not a crunch)
Pulling with arms instead of crunching with abs
Not exhaling forcefully at contraction

days.label: Push
days.title: Push — Chest & Arms Emphasis
days.subtitle: Incline-focused upper chest. Low-to-high cable fly. Extra tricep volume.

exercises.name: Incline DB Press (30-45°)
exercises.tip: 30° hits upper chest best. Lower DBs as deep as mobility allows — the stretch is the growth driver. Elbows 45-60°.
formTips: Set bench to 30° for optimal upper chest activation
Lower dumbbells as deep as mobility allows
Elbows at 45-60° from torso
commonMistakes: Bench angle too steep (becomes a shoulder press)
Not lowering deep enough for full stretch
Flaring elbows to 90°

exercises.name: Low-to-High Cable Fly
exercises.tip: Pulleys at lowest position. Sweep arms up to chin height. Cables keep constant tension where dumbbells lose it.
formTips: Pulleys at lowest position
Sweep arms in an arc up to chin height
Slight forward lean for better chest activation
commonMistakes: Bending elbows too much (turns into a press)
Using too much weight and losing the arc
Not bringing hands high enough

exercises.name: Chest Dips
exercises.tip: 15-20° forward lean = chest dip. Add weight once you hit 3×12 bodyweight.
formTips: Lean forward 15-20° to target chest over triceps
Lower until upper arms are parallel to floor
Controlled descent, explosive push
commonMistakes: Staying too upright (shifts work to triceps)
Not going deep enough
Flaring elbows excessively

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.tip: 5-10° forward lean. Stop at shoulder height. Lead with pinkies. Strict form > heavy weight always.
formTips: Slight 5-10° forward lean
Stop at shoulder height — no higher
Lead with pinkies as if pouring water
commonMistakes: Going above shoulder height (recruits traps)
Using momentum to swing the weight
Going too heavy and losing strict form

exercises.name: OH Tricep Extension (Cable)
exercises.tip: Long head only fully stretches overhead — pushdowns alone won't complete tricep development.
formTips: Get a deep stretch behind your head every rep
Keep upper arms close to your ears
Extend fully and squeeze at the top
commonMistakes: Not getting full stretch at the bottom
Flaring elbows outward
Using too much weight and losing control

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown (Straight Bar)
exercises.tip: Straight bar targets lateral + medial heads. Combined with overhead extension = all three heads covered.
formTips: Lock elbows at your sides throughout
Full extension at the bottom, squeeze triceps
Controlled return — don't let the stack slam
commonMistakes: Elbows drifting forward on each rep
Leaning over the bar and using body weight
Not fully extending at the bottom

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.tip: Progress with 2-5 lb ankle weights rather than more reps for ab hypertrophy.
formTips: Curl pelvis up at the top for ab activation
Add ankle weights to progress rather than more reps
Control the descent — no swinging
commonMistakes: Lifting legs without pelvic tilt (hip flexors dominate)
Swinging and using momentum
Dropping legs uncontrolled

days.label: Pull
days.title: Pull — Back & Arms Emphasis
days.subtitle: Straight-arm pulldown opener. Varied grips. Extra bicep volume.

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.tip: Arms nearly straight. Pre-fatigues lats without tiring biceps — so your back fails before arms on every following exercise.
formTips: Keep arms nearly straight throughout
Slight forward lean at the hips
Focus on pulling with your lats, not arms
commonMistakes: Bending elbows too much (turns into a pulldown)
Standing too upright
Using too much weight and losing lat isolation

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Neutral Grip)
exercises.tip: Neutral grip = lower lat emphasis + more ROM. With Day 1 wide grip = complete lat development.
formTips: Lean back 15° and maintain that angle
Let arms fully extend at the top for stretch
Pull to upper chest
commonMistakes: Not getting full stretch at the top
Using momentum to yank the weight down
Pulling to the stomach instead of upper chest

exercises.name: Cable Row (Wide Angle)
exercises.tip: Pull to upper chest with elbows wide at 90°. Targets rear delts, rhomboids, mid-traps.
formTips: Pull to upper chest with elbows wide at 90°
Squeeze shoulder blades together at peak
Let shoulders protract forward at the stretch
commonMistakes: Pulling to the stomach instead of upper chest
Elbows dropping down (shifts to lats)
Using body momentum to swing

exercises.name: Chest-Supported DB Row
exercises.tip: 30-45° incline bench face down. Eliminates all cheating. Best for mind-muscle connection with back.
formTips: Chest on bench at 30-45° incline
Let arms hang fully at the bottom for stretch
Pull elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades
commonMistakes: Lifting chest off the bench to cheat
Not letting arms fully extend at bottom
Shrugging shoulders instead of retracting

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.tip: Pull to face, split rope, rotate knuckles to ceiling. The rotation is what makes this special.
formTips: Pull rope to face height, then split apart
Externally rotate — knuckles toward ceiling
Pause at peak contraction for 1 second
commonMistakes: Skipping the external rotation at the end
Using too much weight and pulling with body
Pulling to chest instead of face height

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.tip: 10° forward lean increases bottom stretch — stretched position under load is a primary growth driver.
formTips: Slight 10° forward lean for increased stretch
Pin elbows to your sides
Controlled lowering — don't drop the weight
commonMistakes: Swinging the bar up with body momentum
Elbows drifting forward during the curl
Cutting ROM short at the bottom

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.tip: Neutral grip throughout. Brachialis pushes the biceps up when developed — adds arm width.
formTips: Keep neutral grip (thumbs up) throughout
Pin elbows to your sides
Controlled eccentric over 2-3 seconds
commonMistakes: Rotating wrists during the curl
Swinging the dumbbells with momentum
Alternating arms and using body sway

exercises.name: Incline DB Curl
exercises.tip: 45-60° bench, arms behind torso. Only exercise targeting bicep long head in its stretched position.
formTips: Set bench to 45-60°, arms hang behind torso
Keep elbows pinned behind your body throughout
Slow 3-second lowering for maximum stretch
commonMistakes: Elbows drifting forward (loses the stretch)
Using momentum to swing the weight
Bench angle too upright (defeats the purpose)

days.label: Legs
days.title: Legs — Hypertrophy Focus
days.subtitle: Quad-dominant with RDL. Drop sets on isolations. Leg press for loading.

exercises.name: Front Squat / Hack Squat
exercises.tip: More upright = more quad. Hack squat easier to load heavy for pure hypertrophy. Full depth.
formTips: Keep torso as upright as possible
Full depth — hip crease below knees
Knees track over toes throughout
commonMistakes: Leaning forward excessively
Not going deep enough
Letting knees cave inward

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at hips; bar on legs
exercises.tip: Start from rack. Bar drags thighs. 'Hips back' not 'bar down.' Stop when hamstrings stretch.
formTips: Bar drags along your thighs the entire way down
Push hips back — don't just bend forward
Maintain neutral spine — think 'proud chest' throughout
Stop when you feel a deep hamstring stretch
commonMistakes: Rounding the lower back
Bar drifting away from the body
Bending knees too much (turns into a squat)

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.tip: Lower feet = more quad. Higher = more glute. 90° knee bend minimum. Never lock knees.
formTips: Feet shoulder-width, mid-platform
Lower until at least 90° knee bend
Press through full foot, don't let heels lift
commonMistakes: Locking knees at the top
Letting hips lift off the seat at the bottom
Placing feet too high or too low for intended target

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.tip: Last set: failure → drop 30% → failure again. Drop sets recruit remaining fibers.
formTips: Full lockout at the top, squeeze hard
On last set: hit failure, drop 30%, go to failure again
Align knees with machine pivot
commonMistakes: Not fully extending at the top
Using momentum to swing the weight
Dropping too much weight on the drop set

exercises.name: Leg Curl
exercises.tip: Same drop set protocol. RDLs (stretch) + curls (contraction) = complete hamstring development.
formTips: Point toes away to isolate hamstrings
On last set: hit failure, drop 30%, go to failure again
Curl as close to glutes as possible
commonMistakes: Lifting hips off the pad
Rushing through reps on drop set
Not going through full range of motion

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.tip: Straight → toes out 15° → toes in 15° → straight.
formTips: Vary foot angle each set: straight, out, in, straight
Full ROM — drop heels low, rise as high as possible
Pause at both the stretch and contraction
commonMistakes: Bouncing through reps without control
Same foot angle every set
Cutting range of motion short

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.tip: Knees straight targets gastrocnemius (upper calf). Seated targets soleus (lower). You need both for complete calf development. Full ROM — pause at the bottom stretch.
formTips: Keep knees straight throughout — this targets gastrocnemius
Full range of motion: drop heels low, rise as high as possible
Pause 1-2 seconds at the bottom stretch
commonMistakes: Bending knees (shifts work to soleus)
Bouncing through reps
Cutting range of motion short at the bottom

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.tip: Tuck pelvis BEFORE rolling. If lower back arches, you've gone too far.
formTips: Tuck pelvis (posterior tilt) before rolling out
Go only as far as you can without lower back arching
Squeeze abs to pull back, don't use hip flexors
commonMistakes: Lower back arching during the rollout
Going too far out and losing core engagement
Using hips to pull back instead of abs
